source: CIS GOOGLE KUBERNETES ENGINE (GKE) BENCHMARK V1.7.0 PDF
title: Ensure Node Auto-Upgrade is enabled for GKE nodes

input_row.source: CIS GOOGLE KUBERNETES ENGINE (GKE) BENCHMARK V1.7.0 PDF
input_row.title: Ensure Node Auto-Upgrade is enabled for GKE nodes
input_row.description: Node auto-upgrade keeps nodes at the current Kubernetes and OS security patch level to mitigate known vulnerabilities.
input_row.rationale: Node auto-upgrade helps you keep the nodes in the cluster or node pool up to date with the latest stable patch version of Kubernetes as well as the underlying node operating system. Node auto-upgrade uses the same update mechanism as manual node upgrades. Node pools with node auto-upgrade enabled are automatically scheduled for upgrades when a new stable Kubernetes version becomes available. When the upgrade is performed, the Node pool is upgraded to match the current cluster master version. From a security perspective, this has the benefit of applying security updates automatically to the Kubernetes Engine when security fixes are released. Impact: Enabling node auto-upgrade does not cause the nodes to upgrade immediately. Automatic upgrades occur at regular intervals at the discretion of the Kubernetes Engine team. To prevent upgrades occurring during a peak period for the cluster, a maintenance window should be defined. A maintenance window is a four-hour timeframe that can be chosen, during which automatic upgrades should occur. Upgrades can occur on any day of the week, and at any time within the timeframe. To prevent upgrades from occurring during certain dates, a maintenance exclusion should be defined. A maintenance exclusion can span multiple days.
input_row.audit: Using Google Cloud Console 1. Go to Kubernetes Engine by visiting: https://console.cloud.google.com/kubernetes/list 2. From the list of clusters, select the desired cluster. For each Node pool, view the Node pool Details pane and ensure that under the 'Management' heading, 'Auto- upgrade' is set to 'Enabled'. Using Command Line To check the existence of node auto-upgrade for an existing cluster's Node pool, run: gcloud container node-pools describe <node_pool_name> --cluster <cluster_name> --zone <cluster_zone> --format json | jq '.management' Ensure the output of the above command has JSON key attribute autoUpgrade set to true: { "autoUpgrade": true } If node auto-upgrade is disabled, the output of the above command output will not contain the autoUpgrade entry.
input_row.remediation: Using Google Cloud Console 1. Go to Kubernetes Engine by visiting: https://console.cloud.google.com/kubernetes/list. 2. Select the Kubernetes cluster containing the node pool for which auto-upgrade disabled. 3. Select the Node pool by clicking on the name of the pool. 4. Navigate to the Node pool details pane and click EDIT. 5. Under the Management heading, check the Enable auto-repair box. 6. Click SAVE. 7. Repeat steps 2-6 for every cluster and node pool with auto-upgrade disabled. Using Command Line To enable node auto-upgrade for an existing cluster's Node pool, run the following command: gcloud container node-pools update <node_pool_name> --cluster <cluster_name> --zone <cluster_zone> --enable-autoupgrade Default Value: Node auto-upgrade is enabled by default. Even if a cluster has been created with node auto-repair enabled, this only applies to the default Node pool. Subsequent node pools do not have node auto-upgrade enabled by default. References: 1. https://cloud.google.com/kubernetes-engine/docs/concepts/node-auto-upgrades 2. https://cloud.google.com/kubernetes-engine/docs/how-to/maintenance-windows- and-exclusions Additional Information: Node auto-upgrades is not available for Alpha Clusters.
input_row.impact: Enabling node auto-upgrade does not cause the nodes to upgrade immediately. Automatic upgrades occur at regular intervals at the discretion of the Kubernetes Engine team. To prevent upgrades occurring during a peak period for the cluster, a maintenance window should be defined. A maintenance window is a four-hour timeframe that can be chosen, during which automatic upgrades should occur. Upgrades can occur on any day of the week, and at any time within the timeframe. To prevent upgrades from occurring during certain dates, a maintenance exclusion should be defined. A maintenance exclusion can span multiple days.
input_row.references: 1. https://cloud.google.com/kubernetes-engine/docs/concepts/node-auto-upgrades 2. https://cloud.google.com/kubernetes-engine/docs/how-to/maintenance-windows- and-exclusions Additional Information: Node auto-upgrades is not available for Alpha Clusters.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**

The control in question is "Ensure Node Auto-Upgrade is enabled for GKE nodes." The audit instructions provide a specific `gcloud` command to verify the configuration of a GKE node pool's auto-upgrade settings. This transparency indicates that the ideal audit method is automated, as it can be reliably verified through the Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) SDK and does not require manual inspection.

**DECISION CRITERIA:**
- ✓ Can GCP APIs query this property? → Yes, through the use of `gcloud` commands.
- ✓ Is this a standard GCP service property? → Yes, it relates to GKE node management features.
- ✓ Does Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) SDK have methods to check this? → Yes, the `gcloud container node-pools describe` command is available for this purpose.
- ✓ Does GCP CLI have commands to retrieve this? → Yes, it does, as specified by the audit instructions.
- ✓ Does audit mention Google Cloud SDK (gcloud)/GCP CLI commands? → Yes, it provides specific `gcloud` commands.

**PROGRAM NAME:** gcp_gke_node_pool_auto_upgrade_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**

This automated program will check the configuration of GKE node pools for the auto-upgrade setting:

- **GCP API/Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) Method:**
  - Use the `gcloud container node-pools describe` command to retrieve the management configuration for each node pool of a GKE cluster.

- **GCP CLI Command:**
  - `gcloud container node-pools describe <node_pool_name> --cluster <cluster_name> --zone <cluster_zone> --format json`

- **Resources to Enumerate:**
  - Iterate over all clusters and their node pools within the GKE environment.

- **Configuration Properties/Settings to Validate:**
  - Check the `.management.autoUpgrade` setting within the JSON output of each node pool description.

- **Pass/Fail Criteria:**
  - Pass: The `autoUpgrade` attribute is present and set to `true`.
  - Fail: The `autoUpgrade` attribute is absent or set to `false`.

This approach ensures thorough coverage of all clusters and node pools, efficiently identifying any non-compliance with the CIS benchmark requirement.
step2_review:
---

### 1. APPROACH VALIDATION: **AGREE**

- **Manual vs Automated Classification:**  
  The classification as "Automated" is correct. The property in question (`autoUpgrade` for GKE node pools) is directly queryable via GCP APIs and the Google Cloud SDK (`gcloud`). There is no need for manual inspection; all relevant data can be programmatically retrieved and validated.

- **GCP SDK/CLI Capabilities:**  
  The `gcloud container node-pools describe` command exposes the `.management.autoUpgrade` property. Additionally, the GKE REST API (`projects.locations.clusters.nodePools.get`) provides the same information, supporting automation.

- **Other Considerations:**  
  - **GCP Config**: As of this review, GCP Config (formerly Config Connector) does not natively provide a compliance rule for this specific property, so custom automation is appropriate.
  - **CloudFormation:** Not applicable to GCP.

---

### 2. TECHNICAL ACCURACY: **Score: 9/10**

- **Automation Details:**  
  The described automation is technically correct:
  - Enumerate all clusters and node pools.
  - Use `gcloud container node-pools describe` to retrieve node pool configuration.
  - Parse the `.management.autoUpgrade` property.

- **gcloud/CLI Command Validity:**  
  The command is accurate:
  ```
  gcloud container node-pools describe <node_pool_name> --cluster <cluster_name> --zone <cluster_zone> --format=json
  ```
  The `.management.autoUpgrade` field is present in the output.

- **Program Name Convention:**  
  The program name `gcp_gke_node_pool_auto_upgrade_enabled` is clear and descriptive, following the recommended convention.

- **Minor Issues:**  
  - The approach does not mention handling regional clusters, which require the `--region` flag instead of `--zone`.
  - It does not specify how to enumerate all clusters and node pools (e.g., using `gcloud container clusters list` and `gcloud container node-pools list`).
  - No mention of possible API rate limits or pagination.

---

### 3. IMPROVEMENTS

- **Enumerate All Clusters/Node Pools:**  
  Explicitly state that the automation should:
  - Use `gcloud container clusters list --format="..."` to enumerate all clusters (both zonal and regional).
  - Use `gcloud container node-pools list --cluster <cluster_name> --zone <zone or region>` to enumerate all node pools per cluster.

- **Regional Cluster Handling:**  
  - For regional clusters, use `--region` instead of `--zone` in all relevant commands.

- **Alternative GCP API Methods:**  
  - The GKE REST API (`projects.locations.clusters.nodePools.get`) can be used for direct API integration.
  - Cloud Asset Inventory API can be used to inventory node pools at scale.

- **Program Name Suggestion:**  
  - The current name is good, but for even more clarity, consider:  
    `gcp_gke_node_pool_auto_upgrade_enforced`  
    or  
    `gcp_gke_node_pool_auto_upgrade_configured`

- **Additional Best Practices:**  
  - Consider logging clusters/node pools where the property is missing (not just set to `false`).
  - Document how to handle service accounts/permissions required for automation.

---

### 4. CONFIDENCE: **HIGH**

- The assessment is technically sound and aligns with GCP security best practices.
- The automation approach is feasible and effective.
- Minor improvements do not impact the core validity.

---

### 5. FINAL RECOMMENDATION: **Modify**

- **Keep the Automated classification.**
- **Modify** the automation details to:
  - Explicitly include enumeration of all clusters/node pools (zonal and regional).
  - Specify handling of both `--zone` and `--region` flags.
  - Optionally, mention REST API alternatives for completeness.

---

**Summary Table:**

| Section              | Assessment      | Notes                                                                 |
|----------------------|----------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Approach Validation  | AGREE          | Automated is correct; GCP SDK/CLI supports this check                 |
| Technical Accuracy   | 9/10           | Accurate, but lacks regional cluster and enumeration detail           |
| Improvements         | See above      | Add enumeration steps, handle regional clusters, suggest API options  |
| Confidence           | HIGH           | Approach is robust and feasible                                      |
| Final Recommendation | MODIFY         | Refine automation details as noted                                    |

---

step3_final_decision.decision_text:
**FINAL APPROACH:** Automated

**JUSTIFICATION:**  
Both AI opinions correctly identify that the GKE node pool auto-upgrade setting is directly queryable via the Google Cloud SDK (`gcloud`) and GKE REST API. The `.management.autoUpgrade` property is exposed for every node pool, allowing for programmatic, comprehensive, and repeatable compliance checks without manual intervention. The audit steps explicitly reference the use of `gcloud` commands, confirming that automation is both feasible and preferred.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

---

**PROGRAM NAME:**  
gcp_gke_node_pool_auto_upgrade_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- **GCP Resource/Service:** Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE) Node Pools
- **API/CLI Method:**  
  - Google Cloud SDK:  
    `gcloud container node-pools describe <node_pool_name> --cluster <cluster_name> --zone <zone> --format=json`
  - GKE REST API:  
    `projects.locations.clusters.nodePools.get`
- **Properties to Check:**  
  - `.management.autoUpgrade` (Boolean)
- **Pass Criteria:**  
  - The property `management.autoUpgrade` is set to `true` for every node pool in every cluster.
- **Fail Criteria:**  
  - Any node pool where `management.autoUpgrade` is `false` or not set.

---

**DISAGREEMENTS RESOLVED:**  
There were no substantive disagreements between the two AI opinions; both supported automation and provided technically accurate reasoning. My decision confirms and formalizes this consensus.
